zh.lifeSummary: Mabel Tywe是John Tywe的女兒，於大約1230年出生在英國英格蘭賀特福爾華頓 亞特 史東。她和Oliver Daubeny of d'Aubigny至少有1個兒子和1個女兒。她去世於1271年4月5日之後在她的家鄉。

nameMeanings.name: Tywe
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish (Glasgow and Morayshire) and Irish (Wicklow and Dublin): shortened form of   McIntyre  .

variants.name: McIntyre
meanings: Scottish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac an tSaoir ‘son of the craftsman’. Compare Irish McAteer .
History: This is the name of a Scottish clan which, according to clan lore, is descended from Maurice MacNeil, a nephew of Somhairle (English: Sorley or Somerled), King of the Isles (died 1164).

nameMeanings.name: Mabel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a nickname from the Old French vocabulary word amabel, amable ‘lovely’ (akin to modern English amiable ‘friendly, good-humoured’). The initial vowel began to be lost as early as the 12th century (the same woman is referred to as both Mabilia and Amabilia in a document of 1185 ), but a short vowel in the resulting first syllable was standard, giving a rhyme with babble, until the 19th century, when people began to pronounce the name to rhyme with table.

en.lifeSummary: Mabel Tywe was born about 1230, in Watton at Stone, Hertfordshire, England, United Kingdom as the daughter of John Tywe. She had at least 1 son and 1 daughter with Oliver Daubeny of d'Aubigny. She died after 5 April 1271, in her hometown.

ko.lifeSummary: Mabel Tywe(은)는 대략 1230년 영국 잉글랜드 허트퍼드실르 왓튼 앳 스톤에서 John Tywe의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 Oliver Daubeny of d'Aubigny(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1271년 4월 5일 이후 그녀의 고향에서 사망했다.
